mnemonics.title: Kruskal's Algorithm Steps
mnemonics.content: SORT, CHECK, ADD: Sort edges by weight, Check for cycle using Union-Find, Add edge if no cycle. Stop when V-1 edges added.

mnemonics.title: Prim's Algorithm Steps
mnemonics.content: START, FIND, ADD: Start from any vertex, Find minimum edge crossing cut, Add to tree. Repeat until all vertices included.

mnemonics.title: Spanning Tree Properties
mnemonics.content: CANV: Connected, Acyclic, n-1 edges, Vertices all included

acronyms.usage: The spanning tree with minimum total edge weight
Data structure used in Kruskal's algorithm for cycle detection, also called Union-Find
Used in time complexity analysis of spanning tree algorithms
Used in time complexity and edge count (V-1) in spanning trees

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a tree with n nodes hanging from the ceiling. The 'branches' form a connected structure with no loops, touching all nodes exactly once. Count the branches = n-1.
Imagine cutting the graph into two pieces with scissors. The cheapest 'bridge' connecting the two pieces MUST be in the MST.
In a cycle, the most expensive 'toll booth' on the road would never be used in the cheapest route - skip it!
Start with isolated islands. Build bridges one by one, always choosing the cheapest bridge that doesn't create a loop. Stop when all islands are connected.
Start from one island. Keep extending the landmass by attaching the cheapest adjacent island. Keep growing until you own all islands.

analogies.analogy: Like a power grid connecting all cities without any redundant power lines - exactly one path between any two cities.
Planning a road network that connects all cities with minimum total construction cost while still allowing travel between any two cities.
Building a railway network by always laying the cheapest track segment that doesn't create a closed loop.
Growing a tree from a seed - each day adding the nearest branch that extends the tree to new unconnected areas.
When dividing a country into two regions, the cheapest highway connecting them is guaranteed to be in the optimal highway plan.
